<content>
1: class ReactionHandler
2:   class Result
3:     attr_accessor :action, :category, :reaction
4: 
5:     delegate :errors_as_sentence, to: :reaction
6: 
7:     def initialize(reaction: nil, action: nil, category: nil)
8:       @reaction = reaction
9:       @category = category
10:       @action = action
11:     end
12: 
13:     def success?
14:       reaction.errors.none?
15:     end
16:   end
17: 
18:   def self.create(params, current_user:)
19:     new(params, current_user: current_user).create
20:   end
21: 
22:   def self.toggle(params, current_user:)
23:     new(params, current_user: current_user).toggle
24:   end
25: 
26:   def initialize(params, current_user:)
27:     @current_user = current_user
28: 
29:     @params = params
30:     @reactable_id = params[:reactable_id]
31:     @reactable_type = params[:reactable_type]
32:     @category = params[:category] || "like"
33:   end
34: 
35:   attr_reader :category, :params, :current_user, :reactable_type, :reactable_id
36: 
37:   delegate :rate_limiter, to: :current_user
38: 
39:   def create
40:     destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions if %w[Article Comment].include?(reactable_type)
41:     return noop_result if existing_reaction
42: 
43:     create_new_reaction
44:   end
45: 
46:   def toggle
47:     destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions if %w[Article Comment].include?(reactable_type)
48:     return handle_existing_reaction if existing_reaction
49: 
50:     create_new_reaction
51:   end
52: 
53:   private
54: 
55:   def destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions
56:     return unless ReactionCategory[category].privileged?
57: 
58:     reactions = Reaction.contradictory_mod_reactions(
59:       category: category,
60:       reactable_id: reactable_id,
61:       reactable_type: reactable_type,
62:       user: current_user,
63:     )
64:     return if reactions.blank?
65: 
66:     reactions.find_each { |reaction| destroy_reaction(reaction) }
67:   end
68: 
69:   def destroy_reaction(reaction)
70:     destroyed_reaction = Reaction.transaction do
71:       locked = Reaction.lock.find_by(id: reaction.id)
72:       next unless locked
73: 
74:       locked.destroy
75:       locked
76:     end
77: 
78:     return unless destroyed_reaction
79: 
80:     sink_articles(destroyed_reaction)
81:     send_notifications_without_delay(destroyed_reaction)
82:     destroyed_reaction
83:   end
84: 
85:   def existing_reaction
86:   @existing_reaction ||= Reaction.where(
87:     user_id: current_user.id,
88:     reactable_id: reactable_id,
89:     reactable_type: reactable_type,
90:     category: category,
91:   ).first
92:   end
93: 
94:   def create_new_reaction
95:     reaction = build_reaction(category)
96:     result = result(reaction, nil)
97: 
98:     begin
99:       if reaction.save
100:         rate_limit_reaction_creation
101:         sink_articles(reaction)
102:         send_notifications(reaction)
103:         record_feed_event(reaction)
104:         update_last_reacted_at(reaction)
105:       end
106:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
107:       Rails.logger.error "Reaction already exists: #{reaction.inspect}"
108:     end
109: 
110:     result.action = "create"
111: 
112:     if category == "readinglist" && current_user.setting.experience_level
113:       rate_article(reaction)
114:     end
115: 
116:     if current_user.auditable?
117:       Audit::Logger.log(:moderator, current_user, params.dup)
118:     end
119: 
120:     result
121:   end
122: 
123:   def build_reaction(category)
124:     create_params = {
125:       user_id: current_user.id,
126:       reactable_id: reactable_id,
127:       reactable_type: reactable_type,
128:       category: category
129:     }
130:     if (current_user&.any_admin? || current_user&.super_moderator?) &&
131:         ReactionCategory[category].negative?
132:       create_params[:status] = "confirmed"
133:     end
134:     Reaction.new(create_params)
135:   end
136: 
137:   def handle_existing_reaction
138:     locked_reaction = destroy_reaction(existing_reaction)
139:     log_audit(locked_reaction) if locked_reaction
140:     result(existing_reaction, "destroy")
141:   end
142: 
143:   def log_audit(reaction)
144:     return unless reaction.negative? && current_user.auditable?
145: 
146:     updated_params = params.dup
147:     updated_params[:action] = "destroy"
148:     Audit::Logger.log(:moderator, current_user, updated_params)
149:   end
150: 
151:   def result(reaction, action)
152:     Result.new category: category, reaction: reaction, action: action
153:   end
154: 
155:   def rate_limit_reaction_creation
156:     rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:reaction_creation)
157:   end
158: 
159:   def sink_articles(reaction)
160:     Moderator::SinkArticles.call(reaction.reactable_id) if vomit_on_user?
161:   end
162: 
163:   def send_notifications(reaction)
164:     Notification.send_reaction_notification(reaction, reaction.target_user)
165:     return unless reaction.reaction_on_organization_article?
166: 
167:     Notification.send_reaction_notification(reaction, reaction.reactable.organization)
168:   end
169: 
170:   def send_notifications_without_delay(reaction)
171:     Notification.send_reaction_notification_without_delay(reaction, reaction.target_user)
172:     return unless reaction.reaction_on_organization_article?
173: 
174:     Notification.send_reaction_notification_without_delay(reaction, reaction.reactable.organization)
175:   end
176: 
177:   def record_feed_event(reaction)
178:     return unless (reaction.visible_to_public? || reaction.category == "readinglist") &&
179:       reaction.reactable_type == "Article"
180: 
181:     FeedEvent.record_journey_for(reaction.user, article: reaction.reactable, category: :reaction)
182:   end
183: 
184:   def rate_article(reaction)
185:     user_experience_level = current_user.setting.experience_level
186:     return unless user_experience_level
187: 
188:     RatingVote.create(article_id: reaction.reactable_id,
189:                       group: "experience_level",
190:                       user_id: current_user.id,
191:                       context: "readinglist_reaction",
192:                       rating: user_experience_level)
193:   end
194: 
195:   def noop_result
196:     Result.new category: category, action: "none", reaction: existing_reaction
197:   end
198: 
199:   def vomit_on_user?
200:     reactable_type == "User" && category == "vomit"
201:   end
202: 
203:   def update_last_reacted_at(reaction)
204:     reaction.user.touch(:last_reacted_at)
205:   end
206: end

9:       references = LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: ["Article", nil], referenced_id: article_id)
10:       
11:       if article&.organization_id.present?
12:         references = references.or(LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: "Organization", referenced_id: article.organization_id, tag_name: "org_posts"))

23:           if original_html != record.processed_html

25:           end
26:         elsif record.respond_to?(:evaluate_markdown, true) && record.respond_to?(:processed_html=)
27:           record.send(:evaluate_markdown)
28:           if original_html != record.processed_html
29:             record.update_column(:processed_html, record.processed_html)
30:             
31:             if record.respond_to?(:async_bust)

33:             elsif record.respond_to?(:bust_cache)

1: module Articles
2:   class HandleSpamWorker
3:     include Sidekiq::Job
4: 
5:     sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority
6: 
7:     def perform(article_id)
8:       article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
9:       return unless article
10: 
11:       # Always run spam handling and score update
12:       Spam::Handler.handle_article!(article: article)
13:       article.reload.update_score
14: 
15:       # Only enhance if clickbait score is 0 (not already processed) and score is >= 0 (good quality after spam check)
16:       return unless article.clickbait_score.zero? && article.score >= 0
17: 
18:       enhance_article(article)
19:     end
20: 
21:     private
22: 
23:     def enhance_article(article)
24:       # Create AI client for dependency injection
25:       ai_client = Ai::Base.new
26:       enhancer = Ai::ArticleEnhancer.new(article, ai_client: ai_client)
27: 
28:       # Calculate and update clickbait score
29:       clickbait_score = enhancer.calculate_clickbait_score
30:       article.update_column(:clickbait_score, clickbait_score)
31: 
32:       # Generate and apply tags if article has no tags and meets criteria
33:       if should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
34:         suggested_tags = enhancer.generate_tags
35:         apply_tags_to_article(article, suggested_tags) if suggested_tags.any?
36:       end
37:     rescue StandardError => e
38:       Rails.logger.error("Article enhancement failed for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
39:     end
40: 
41:     def should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
42:       article.cached_tag_list.blank? &&
43:         article.score >= 0 &&
44:         clickbait_score < 0.6
45:     end
46: 
47:     def apply_tags_to_article(article, tag_names)
48:       # Validate that all suggested tags exist and are supported
49:       valid_tags = Tag.supported.where(name: tag_names).pluck(:name)
50: 
51:       if valid_tags.any?
52:         # Apply tags to article
53:         article.tag_list = valid_tags
54:         article.cached_tag_list = valid_tags.join(", ")
55:         article.save
56: 
57:         Rails.logger.info("Applied tags #{valid_tags.join(', ')} to article #{article.id}")
58:       else
59:         Rails.logger.warn("No valid tags found from suggestions #{tag_names.join(', ')} for article #{article.id}")
60:       end
61:     end
62:   end
63: end

1: module Articles
2:   # Maintains the per-article ArticleActivity cache row by applying a single
3:   # event delta (page view / reaction / comment) instead of re-querying the
4:   # whole day. If the article has no activity row yet, one is created and a
5:   # full backfill from raw rows runs — this is the "upsert" referenced in the
6:   # design. Subsequent events then take the fast append path.
7:   #
8:   # Args:
9:   #   article_id : Integer
10:   #   event_type : "page_view" | "reaction" | "comment" | nil (nil = full recompute)
11:   #   action     : "create" | "destroy"
12:   #   payload    : Hash of attributes carried inline so destroys work even
13:   #                after the source row is gone. Keys vary by event_type:
14:   #     page_view: { iso, total, sum_read_seconds, logged_in_count, domain }
15:   #     reaction:  { iso, category, user_id }
16:   #     comment:   { iso, score }
17:   class UpdateArticleActivityWorker
18:     include Sidekiq::Job
19: 
20:     # Deltas are non-idempotent — applying the same job twice would
21:     # double-count (or, on destroy, drive totals negative). retry: false
22:     # accepts that a transient failure loses one event; the next full
23:     # recompute (lazy upsert path or manual repair) reconciles drift.
24:     # We use sidekiq-unique-jobs to ensure at most one job is enqueued/scheduled.
25:     sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority,
26:                     retry: false,
27:                     lock: :until_executing,
28:                     on_conflict: :replace
29: 
30:     class << self
31:       def perform_async(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
32:         return if article_id.nil?
33: 
34:         # Fetch last_aggregated_at, page_views_count, and created_at in a single fast query
35:         article_data = Article.left_outer_joins(:article_activity)
36:                               .where(id: article_id)
37:                               .pluck(:created_at, :page_views_count, "article_activities.last_aggregated_at")
38:                               .first
39:         return if article_data.nil?
40: 
41:         created_at, page_views_count, last_aggregated_at = article_data
42: 
43:         delay = debounce_delay_for(page_views_count.to_i, created_at)
44: 
45:         if last_aggregated_at.nil?
46:           # If never aggregated, run immediately
47:           super(article_id)
48:         else
49:           elapsed = Time.current - last_aggregated_at
50:           if elapsed >= delay
51:             # If it has been longer than the delay, run immediately
52:             super(article_id)
53:           else
54:             # Schedule it for the remaining delay time
55:             # Since lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace is active,
56:             # this will replace/coalesce any already scheduled job for this article.
57:             perform_in(delay - elapsed, article_id)
58:           end
59:         end
60:       end
61: 
62:       def debounce_delay_for(page_views, created_at)
63:         base_delay = if page_views >= 100_000
64:                        5.minutes
65:                      elsif page_views >= 10_000
66:                        1.minute
67:                      elsif page_views >= 1_000
68:                        30.seconds
69:                      else
70:                        10.seconds
71:                      end
72: 
73:         return base_delay if created_at.nil?
74: 
75:         # Exponential decay/backoff based on age (up to 30 days to avoid float overflow)
76:         days_old = [[(Time.current - created_at) / 1.day, 0].max, 30].min
77:         calculated_delay = base_delay * (1.5**days_old)
78: 
79:         [calculated_delay, 30.minutes].min
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def perform(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
84:       return if article_id.nil?
85: 
86:       activity = ArticleActivity.find_by(article_id: article_id)
87: 
88:       if activity.nil?
89:         return unless Article.exists?(id: article_id)
90: 
91:         activity = ArticleActivity.find_or_create_by!(article_id: article_id)
92:         activity.recompute_all! if activity.previously_new_record?
93:         return
94:       end
95: 
96:       # Always recompute on execution so legacy queued jobs with delta-style
97:       # args converge with newer article_id-only jobs. This avoids drift when
98:       # an older non-idempotent delta job runs after a recompute/coalesced job.
99:       activity.recompute_all!
100:     end
101:   end
102: end

(End of file - total 102 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: class ArticlesController < ApplicationController
2:   include ApplicationHelper
3: 
4:   # NOTE: It seems quite odd to not authenticate the user for the :new action.
5:   before_action :authenticate_user!, except: %i[feed new]
6:   before_action :set_article, only: %i[edit manage update destroy stats admin_unpublish admin_featured_toggle]
7:   # NOTE: Consider pushing this check into the associated Policy.  We could choose to raise a
8:   #       different error which we could then rescue as part of our exception handling.
9:   before_action :check_suspended, only: %i[new create update]
10:   before_action :set_cache_control_headers, only: %i[feed]
11:   after_action :verify_authorized
12: 
13:   ##
14:   # [@jeremyf] - My dreamiest of dreams is to move this to the ApplicationController.  But it's very
15:   #              presence could create some havoc with our edge caching.  So I'm scoping it to the
16:   #              place where the code is likely to raise an ApplicationPolicy::UserRequiredError.
17:   #
18:   #              I still want to enable this, but first want to get things mostly conformant with
19:   #              existing expectations.  Note, in config/application.rb, we're rescuing the below
20:   #              exception as though it was a Pundit::NotAuthorizedError.
21:   #
22:   #              The difference being that rescue_from is an ALWAYS use case.  Whereas the
23:   #              config/application.rb uses the config.consider_all_requests_local to determine if
24:   #              we bubble the exception up or handle it.
25:   #
26:   # rescue_from ApplicationPolicy::UserRequiredError, with: :respond_with_request_for_authentication
27: 
28:   def feed
29:     # [@jeremyf] - I am a firm believer that we should check authorization.  However, in this case,
30:     #              based on our implementation constraints and assumptions, the `#feed` action will
31:     #              almost certainly be available to everyone (what's in the feed will vary
32:     #              signficantly).  So while I would love an `authorize(Article)` here, I will make
33:     #              do with a comment.
34:     skip_authorization
35: 
36:     @articles = Article.feed.order(published_at: :desc).page(params[:page].to_i).per(12)
37:     @latest = request.path == latest_feed_path
38:     @articles = if params[:username]
39:                   handle_user_or_organization_feed
40:                 elsif params[:tag]
41:                   handle_tag_feed
42:                 elsif @latest
43:                   @articles
44:                     .where("score > ?", Articles::Feeds::Latest::MINIMUM_SCORE)
45:                     .includes(:user)
46:                 else
47:                   @articles
48:                     .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
49:                     .includes(:user)
50:                 end
51: 
52:     not_found unless @articles&.any?
53: 
54:     user_keys = if @user&.respond_to?(:profile_identity_cache_keys)
55:                   @user.profile_identity_cache_keys
56:                 else
57:                   Array.wrap(@user&.record_key)
58:                 end
59:     set_surrogate_key_header(*[user_keys, @articles.map(&:record_key)].flatten.compact)
60:     set_cache_control_headers(10.minutes.to_i, stale_while_revalidate: 30, stale_if_error: 1.day.to_i)
61: 
62:     render layout: false, content_type: "application/xml", locals: {
63:       articles: @articles,
64:       user: @user,
65:       tag: @tag,
66:       latest: @latest,
67:       allowed_tags: MarkdownProcessor::AllowedTags::FEED,
68:       allowed_attributes: MarkdownProcessor::AllowedAttributes::FEED,
69:       scrubber: FeedMarkdownScrubber.new
70:     }
71:   end
72: 
73:   # @note The /new path is a unique creature.  We want to ensure that folks coming to the /new with
74:   #       a prefill of information are first prompted to sign-in, and then given a form that
75:   #       prepopulates with that pre-fill information.  This is a feature that StackOverflow and
76:   #       CodePen use to have folks post on Dev.
77:   def new
78:     base_editor_assignments
79: 
80:     @article, needs_authorization = Articles::Builder.call(@user, @tag, @prefill)
81: 
82:     if needs_authorization
83:       authorize(Article)
84:     else
85:       skip_authorization
86: 
87:       # We want the query params for the request (as that is where we have the prefill).  The
88:       # `request.path` excludes the query parameters, so we're going with the `request.url` which
89:       # includes the parameters.
90:       store_location_for(:user, request.url)
91:     end
92:   end
93: 
94:   def edit
95:     authorize @article
96: 
97:     @version = @article.has_frontmatter? ? "v1" : "v2"
98:     @user = @article.user
99:     @organizations = @user&.organizations
100:     @user_approved_liquid_tags = Users::ApprovedLiquidTags.call(@user)
101:   end
102: 
103:   def manage
104:     authorize @article
105: 
106:     @article = @article.decorate
107:     @discussion_lock = @article.discussion_lock
108:     @user = @article.user
109:     @organizations = @user&.organizations
110:     # TODO: fix this for multi orgs
111:     @org_members = @organization.users.pluck(:name, :id) if @organization
112:   end
113: 
114:   def preview
115:     authorize Article
116: 
117:     begin
118:       renderer = ContentRenderer.new(params[:article_body], source: Article.new, user: current_user)
119:       result = renderer.process_article
120:       processed_html = result.processed_html
121:       front_matter = result.front_matter.to_h
122:     rescue StandardError => e
123:       @article = Article.new(body_markdown: params[:article_body])
124:       @article.errors.add(:base, ErrorMessages::Clean.call(e.message))
125:     end
126: 
127:     respond_to do |format|
128:       if @article
129:         format.json { render json: @article.errors, status: :unprocessable_entity }
130:       else
131:         format.json do
132:           if front_matter["tags"]
133:             tags = Article.new.tag_list.add(front_matter["tags"], parser: ActsAsTaggableOn::TagParser)
134:           end
135:           if front_matter["cover_image"]
136:             cover_image = ApplicationController.helpers.cloud_cover_url(front_matter["cover_image"])
137:           end
138: 
139:           render json: {
140:             processed_html: processed_html,
141:             title: front_matter["title"],
142:             tags: tags,
143:             cover_image: cover_image

1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     class LargeForemExperimental
4:       def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil)
5:         @user = user
6:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
7:         @page = page
8:         @tag = tag
9:         @article_score_applicator = Articles::Feeds::ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser.new(user: user)
10:       end
11: 
12:       def default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false)
13:         _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: user_signed_in, ranking: true)
14:         stories
15:       end
16: 
17:       # @param user_signed_in [Boolean] are we treating this as an
18:       #        anonymous user?
19:       # @param ranking [Boolean] if true, apply a ranking algorithm
20:       # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
21:       #        story must have a main image
22:       #
23:       # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
24:       def featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false, ranking: true, must_have_main_image: true)
25:         featured_story, hot_stories = globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
26:         hot_stories = rank_and_sort_articles(hot_stories) if @user && ranking
27:         [featured_story, hot_stories]
28:       end
29: 
30:       # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.
31:       # Eventually, we should be able to remove the alias.
32:       alias default_home_feed_and_featured_story featured_story_and_default_home_feed
33: 
34:       def more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
35:         _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: true)
36:         first_quarter(stories).shuffle + last_three_quarters(stories)
37:       end
38: 
39:       # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.  However,
40:       # in this code base there are no further references of
41:       # :more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end
42:       alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
43: 
44:       # @api private
45:       def rank_and_sort_articles(articles)
46:         ranked_articles = articles.each_with_object({}) do |article, result|
47:           article_points = score_single_article(article)
48:           result[article] = article_points
49:         end
50:         ranked_articles = ranked_articles.sort_by { |_article, article_points| -article_points }.map(&:first)
51:         ranked_articles.to(@number_of_articles - 1)
52:       end
53: 
54:       # @api private
55:       def score_single_article(article, base_article_points: 0)
56:         article_points = base_article_points
57:         article_points += score_followed_user(article)
58:         article_points += score_followed_organization(article)
59:         article_points += score_followed_tags(article)
60:         article_points += score_experience_level(article)
61:         article_points += score_comments(article)
62:         article_points
63:       end
64: 
65:       delegate(:score_followed_user,
66:                :score_followed_tags,
67:                :score_followed_organization,
68:                :score_experience_level,
69:                :score_comments,
70:                to: :@article_score_applicator)
71: 
72:       # @api private
73:       # rubocop:disable Layout/LineLength
74:       def globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: true, article_score_threshold: -15, min_rand_limit: 15, max_rand_limit: 80)
75:         # rubocop:enable Layout/LineLength
76:         if user_signed_in
77:           hot_stories = experimental_hot_story_grab
78:           hot_stories = hot_stories.not_authored_by(UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id))
79:           featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
80:           new_stories = Article.published.from_subforem
81:             .where("score > ?", article_score_threshold)
82:             .limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
83:             .order(published_at: :desc)
84:             .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
85:             .limit(rand(min_rand_limit..max_rand_limit))
86:           hot_stories = hot_stories.to_a + new_stories.to_a
87:         else
88:           hot_stories = Article.published.from_subforem.limited_column_select
89:             .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, top_comments: :user)
90:             .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
91:             .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
92:             .order(hotness_score: :desc)
93:           featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
94:         end
95:         [featured_story, hot_stories.to_a]
96:       end
97: 
98:       private
99: 
100:       def featured_story_from(stories:, must_have_main_image:)
101:         return stories.first unless must_have_main_image
102: 
103:         stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
104:       end
105: 
106:       def experimental_hot_story_grab
107:         start_time = Articles::Feeds.oldest_published_at_to_consider_for(user: @user)
108:         Article.published.limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
109:           .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
110:           .where("published_at > ?", start_time)
111:           .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
112:           .order(score: :desc)
113:       end
114: 
115:       def first_quarter(array)
116:         array[0...(array.length / 4)]
117:       end
118: 
119:       def last_three_quarters(array)
120:         array[(array.length / 4)..array.length]
121:       end
122:     end
123:   end
124: end

1: module Articles
2:   # This module is responsible for updating a specific page view for a given article and user.
3:   #
4:   # @see Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker for the sibling that's responsible for recording page
5:   #      views.
6:   module PageViewUpdater
7:     EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER = 60
8:     # @param article_id [Integer]
9:     # @param user_id [Integer]
10:     #
11:     # @return [TrueClass] if we updated or created a PageView
12:     # @return [FalseClass] if we did not update a PageView
13:     #
14:     # @note Regardless of return status, consider the business logic successful unless we raise an
15:     #       exception.  The return value is present for easing testing.  The `find_or_create_by`
16:     #       adds a complication in the testing logic
17:     #       (e.g., `expect { Articles::PageViewUpdater.call }.not_to change(PageView, :count) `
18:     def self.call(article_id:, user_id:)
19:       # Don't record views to non-existent or unpublished articles.
20:       return false unless Article.published.exists?(id: article_id)
21:       # Don't record author's own views.
22:       return false if Article.published.from_subforem.exists?(id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
23: 
24:       page_view = PageView.order(created_at: :desc)
25:         .find_or_create_by(article_id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
26:       return true if page_view.new_record?
27: 
28:       new_time_mark = page_view.time_tracked_in_seconds + 15
29:       page_view.update_column(:time_tracked_in_seconds, new_time_mark)
30:       if new_time_mark == EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER
31:         FeedEvent.record_journey_for(page_view.user, article: page_view.article, category: :extended_pageview)
32:         UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(user_id, article_id, 0.05) if user_id
33:       end
34: 
35:       true
36:     end
37:   end
38: end

5:     def self.search_documents(
6:       term: nil,
7:       user_id: nil,

9:       sort_direction: nil,
10:       page: nil,
11:       per_page: nil
12:     )
13:       relation = Homepage::ArticlesQuery.call(user_id: user_id, page: page, per_page: per_page)
14: 
15:       relation = relation.search_articles(term) if term.present?
16: 
17:       relation = sort(relation, term, sort_by, sort_direction)
18: 
19:       Homepage::ArticleSerializer.serialized_collection_from(relation: relation)
20:     end
21: 
22:     def self.sort(relation, term, sort_by, sort_direction)
23:       # By skipping ordering, we rely on the custom ranking defined in the article's tsvector document
24:       return relation if term.present? && sort_by.blank?
25: 
26:       return relation.reorder(sort_by => sort_direction) if sort_by&.to_sym == :published_at && sort_direction
27: 
28:       relation.reorder(DEFAULT_SORT_BY)
29:     end
30:     private_class_method :sort
31:   end
32: end

1: module Homepage
2:   class ArticlesQuery
3:     ATTRIBUTES = %i[
4:       cached_tag_list
5:       comments_count
6:       crossposted_at
7:       displayed_comments_count
8:       id
9:       organization_id
10:       path
11:       public_reactions_count
12:       published_at
13:       reading_time
14:       title
15:       type_of
16:       user_id
17:       video_duration_in_seconds
18:       video_thumbnail_url
19:     ].freeze
20:     DEFAULT_PER_PAGE = 60
21:     MAX_PER_PAGE = 100
22: 
23:     SORT_PARAMS = %i[hotness_score public_reactions_count published_at].freeze
24:     DEFAULT_SORT_DIRECTION = :desc
25: 
26:     def self.call(...)
27:       new(...).call
28:     end
29: 
30:     # TODO: [@rhymes] change frontend to start from page 1
31:     def initialize(
32:       approved: nil,
33:       published_at: nil,
34:       user_id: nil,
35:       organization_id: nil,
36:       tags: [],
37:       hidden_tags: [],
38:       sort_by: nil,
39:       sort_direction: nil,
40:       page: 0,
41:       per_page: DEFAULT_PER_PAGE
42:     )
43:       @relation = Article.published.from_subforem.select(*ATTRIBUTES)
44:         .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories)
45: 
46:       @approved = approved
47:       @published_at = published_at
48:       @user_id = user_id
49:       @organization_id = organization_id
50:       @tags = tags.presence || []
51:       @hidden_tags = hidden_tags.presence || []
52: 
53:       @sort_by = sort_by
54:       @sort_direction = sort_direction || DEFAULT_SORT_DIRECTION
55: 
56:       @page = page.to_i + 1
57:       @per_page = [(per_page || DEFAULT_PER_PAGE).to_i, MAX_PER_PAGE].min
58:     end
59: 
60:     def call
61:       filter.merge(sort).merge(paginate)
62:     end
63: 
64:     private
65: 
66:     attr_reader :relation, :approved, :published_at, :user_id, :organization_id, :tags, :hidden_tags,
67:                 :sort_by, :sort_direction, :page, :per_page
68: 
69:     def filter
70:       @relation = @relation.full_posts
71:       @relation = @relation.where(approved: approved) unless approved.nil?
72:       @relation = @relation.where(published_at: published_at) if published_at.present?
73:       @relation = @relation.where(user_id: user_id) if user_id.present?
74:       @relation = @relation.where(organization_id: organization_id) if organization_id.present?
75:       @relation = @relation.cached_tagged_with_any(tags) if tags.any?
76:       @relation = @relation.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags) if hidden_tags.any?
77:       @relation = @relation.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories)
78:       @relation = @relation.where("score >= 0") # Never return negative score articles
79:       @relation = @relation.from_subforem
80: 
81:       relation
82:     end
83: 
84:     def sort
85:       return relation unless SORT_PARAMS.include?(sort_by&.to_sym)
86: 
87:       relation.order(sort_by => sort_direction)
88:     end
89: 
90:     def paginate
91:       relation.page(page).per(per_page)
92:     end
93:   end
94: end

1: module Feeds
2:   # Responsible for fetching RSS feeds for multiple feed sources.
3:   #
4:   # @see Feeds::Import.call
5:   class Import
6:     # Fetch the feeds for the given scope of feed sources.
7:     #
8:     # @param users_scope [ActiveRecord::Relation<User>] the initial scope for determining which
9:     #        users' feed sources we'll be fetching.
10:     #
11:     # @param earlier_than [NilClass, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone] when given, use this to further
12:     #        filter which feed sources to fetch (based on last_fetched_at).
13:     #
14:     # @return [Integer] count of total articles fetched.
15:     def self.call(users_scope: User, earlier_than: nil)
16:       new(users_scope: users_scope, earlier_than: earlier_than).call
17:     end
18: 
19:     def initialize(users_scope: User, earlier_than: nil)
20:       @earlier_than = earlier_than
21:       @feed_sources = filter_feed_sources(users_scope: users_scope, earlier_than: earlier_than)
22: 
23:       # NOTE: should these be configurable? Currently they are the result of empiric
24:       # tests trying to find a balance between memory occupation and speed
25:       @sources_batch_size = 50
26:       @num_fetchers = 8
27:       @num_parsers = 4
28:     end
29: 
30:     def call
31:       total_articles_count = 0
32: 
33:       feed_sources.in_batches(of: sources_batch_size) do |batch_of_sources|
34:         sources = batch_of_sources.includes(:user, :organization, :author).to_a
35:         sources_by_id = sources.index_by(&:id)
36: 
37:         fetch_results = fetch_feeds(sources, sources_by_id)
38:         feeds_per_source_id = fetch_results[:feeds]
39: 
40:         feedjira_objects, parse_failures = parse_feeds(feeds_per_source_id)
41: 
42:         # Record fetch failures
43:         record_fetch_failures(fetch_results[:failures], sources_by_id)
44: 
45:         # Record parse failures
46:         record_parse_failures(parse_failures, sources_by_id)
47: 
48:         # NOTE: doing this sequentially to avoid locking problems with the DB
49:         articles = feedjira_objects.flat_map do |source_id, feed|
50:           source = sources_by_id[source_id]
51:           create_articles_from_feed_source(source, feed)
52:         end
53: 
54:         total_articles_count += articles.length
55: 
56:         # Update last_fetched_at on each source and the user's feed_fetched_at for backward compat
57:         now = Time.current
58:         batch_of_sources.update_all(last_fetched_at: now)
59:         user_ids = sources.map(&:user_id).uniq
60:         User.where(id: user_ids).update_all(feed_fetched_at: now)
61:       end
62: 
63:       total_articles_count
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     attr_reader :earlier_than, :sources_batch_size, :num_fetchers, :num_parsers, :feed_sources
69: 
70:     def filter_feed_sources(users_scope:, earlier_than:)
71:       authorized_user_ids = ArticlePolicy
72:         .scope_users_authorized_to_action(users_scope: users_scope, action: :create)
73:         .select(:id)
74: 
75:       recent_activity_since = 3.months.ago
76:       active_user_ids = User
77:         .where(id: authorized_user_ids)
78:         .where("last_article_at >= ? OR last_presence_at >= ?",
79:                recent_activity_since, recent_activity_since)
80:         .select(:id)
81: 
82:       sources = Feeds::Source.active.where(user_id: active_user_ids)
83: 
84:       return sources unless earlier_than
85: 
86:       sources.where(last_fetched_at: nil).or(sources.where(last_fetched_at: ..earlier_than))
87:     end
88: 
89:     def fetch_feeds(sources, sources_by_id)
90:       data = sources.map { |s| [s.id, s.feed_url] }
91:       failures = []
92: 
93:       result = Parallel.map(data, in_threads: num_fetchers) do |source_id, url|
94:         cleaned_url = url.to_s.strip
95:         next if cleaned_url.blank?
96: 
97:         response = fetch_with_retry(cleaned_url)
98: 
99:         [source_id, response.body]
100:       rescue StandardError => e
101:         source = sources_by_id[source_id]
102:         report_error(
103:           e,
104:           feeds_import_info: {
105:             source_id: source_id,
106:             user_id: source&.user_id,
107:             url: url,
108:             error: "Feeds::Import::FetchFeedError"
109:           },
110:         )
111: 
112:         failures << { source_id: source_id, feed_url: url, error: sanitize_error(e.message) }
113:         next
114:       end
115: 
116:       { feeds: result.compact.to_h, failures: failures }
117:     end
118: 
119:     def parse_feeds(feeds_per_source_id)
120:       failures = []
121: 
122:       result = Parallel.map(feeds_per_source_id, in_threads: num_parsers) do |source_id, feed_xml|
123:         parsed_feed = Feedjira.parse(feed_xml)
124: 
125:         [source_id, parsed_feed]
126:       rescue StandardError => e
127:         report_error(
128:           e,
129:           feeds_import_info: {
130:             source_id: source_id,
131:             error: "Feeds::Import::ParseFeedError"
132:           },
133:         )
134: 
135:         failures << { source_id: source_id, error: sanitize_error(e.message) }
136:         next
137:       end
138: 
139:       [result.compact.to_h, failures]
140:     end
141: 
142:     def create_articles_from_feed_source(feed_source, feed)
143:       start_time = Time.current
144:       articles = []
145:       user = feed_source.user

1: module Spam
2:   # This module is responsible for handling spam in our various user input sources.
3:   #
4:   # @note We may not immediately block spam but instead slowly escalate our response.
5:   module Handler
6:     # These are purely words that can help trigger an investigation.
7:     # They are never to be used to directly take any action.
8:     # They can absolutely be used out of context and only exist to trigger investigation.
9:     # Depending on the forem, they are hypothetically not even abuse in any way.
10:     PROFILE_SPAM_TRIGGER_TERMS = [
11:       "buy links",
12:       "buying links",
13:       "backlinks",
14:       "link building",
15:       "seo services",
16:       "casino",
17:       "gambling",
18:       "betting",
19:       "escort",
20:       "prostitut",
21:       "adult",
22:       "girls",
23:       "porn",
24:       "onlyfans",
25:       "crypto pump",
26:       "forex signals",
27:       "loan shark",
28:     ].freeze
29:     # @return [TrueClass] if we are going to try to use more rigorous spam handling
30:     # @return [FalseClass] if we are using less rigorous spam handling
31:     def self.more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?
32:       FeatureFlag.enabled?(:more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking)
33:     end
34: 
35:     # @return [TrueClass] if we are going to unpublish articles when we auto-suspend
36:     # @return [FalseClass] if we are not going to unpublish articles when we auto-suspend
37:     def self.unpublish_all_posts_when_user_auto_suspended?
38:       FeatureFlag.enabled?(:unpublish_all_posts_when_user_auto_suspended)
39:     end
40: 
41:     # Test the article for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
42:     #
43:     # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
44:     #
45:     # @param article [Article] the article to check for spamminess
46:     # @param attributes [Array<Symbol>] test these attributes of the article.
47:     def self.handle_article!(article:, attributes: %i[title body_markdown])
48:       if article_linked_domain_spam?(article)
49:         article.update_column(:automod_label, "clear_and_obvious_spam")
50:         article.automod_label = "clear_and_obvious_spam"
51:       else
52:         # First, run content moderation labeling
53:         label_article_content!(article)
54:       end
55: 
56:       # Handle clear and obvious violations immediately
57:       if %w[clear_and_obvious_spam clear_and_obvious_harmful clear_and_obvious_inciting].include?(article.automod_label)
58:         issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: article)
59: 
60:         if Reaction.user_has_been_given_too_many_spammy_article_reactions?(
61:           user: article.user,
62:           include_user_profile: more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?,
63:         )
64:           suspend!(user: article.user)
65:         end
66: 
67:         return :spam
68:       end
69: 
70:       # High quality content bypasses spam checks entirely
71:       if %w[very_good_and_on_topic great_and_on_topic very_good_but_offtopic_for_subforem
72:             great_but_off_topic_for_subforem].include?(article.automod_label)
73:         return :not_spam
74:       end
75: 
76:       # For likely violations, bypass badge count and other restrictions but still run checks
77:       bypass_restrictions = %w[likely_spam likely_harmful likely_inciting].include?(article.automod_label)
78: 
79:       # Continue with existing spam detection logic
80:       text = attributes.map { |attr| article.public_send(attr) }.join("\n")
81: 
82:       # Check if we should trigger spam detection
83:       should_check = Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: text) ||
84:         (article.processed_html.include?("<a") && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present? &&
85:          (bypass_restrictions || article.user.badge_achievements_count < 4) &&
86:          Ai::ArticleCheck.new(article).spam?)
87: 
88:       return :not_spam unless should_check
89: 
90:       issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: article)
91: 
92:       return unless Reaction.user_has_been_given_too_many_spammy_article_reactions?(
93:         user: article.user,
94:         include_user_profile: more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?,
95:       )
96: 
97:       suspend!(user: article.user)
98:     end
99: 
100:     # Test the comment for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
101:     #
102:     # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
103:     #
104:     # @param comment [Comment] the comment to check for spamminess
105:     def self.handle_comment!(comment:)
106:       # Existing checks for trusted users.
107:       return :not_spam if comment.user.badge_achievements_count > 6
108:       return :not_spam if comment.user.base_subscriber?
109: 
110:       if (domain = extract_first_domain_from(comment.processed_html)) && extensive_domain_spam?(domain: domain,
111:                                                                                                 current_comment: comment)
112:         issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: comment)
113:         suspend_if_user_is_repeat_offender(user: comment.user)
114:         return :spam # Return early as it's confirmed spam.
115:       end
116: 
117:       rate_limit_spam = Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: comment.body_markdown)
118: 
119:       # Return if neither of the spam conditions are met.
120:       return :not_spam unless rate_limit_spam ||
121:         (comment.processed_html.include?("<a") && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present? && Ai::CommentCheck.new(comment).spam?)
122: 
123:       issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: comment)
124:       suspend_if_user_is_repeat_offender(user: comment.user)
125:     end
126: 
127:     # Test the user for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
128:     #
129:     # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
130:     #
131:     # @param user [User] the user to check for spamminess
132:     def self.handle_user!(user:)
133:       text = [user.name]
134: 
135:       if more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?
136:         text += [
137:           user.email,
138:           user.github_username,
139:           user.profile&.website_url,
140:           user.profile&.location,
141:           user.profile&.summary,
142:           user.twitter_username,
143:           user.username,
144:         ].compact
145:       end
146: 
147:       text = text.join("\n")
148: 
149:       return :not_spam unless Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: text)

1: module Comments
2:   class CalculateScore
3:     def self.call(...)
4:       new(...).call
5:     end
6: 
7:     def initialize(comment)
8:       @comment = comment
9:     end
10: 
11:     def call
12:       score = BlackBox.comment_quality_score(comment)
13:       score -= 500 if comment.user&.spam?
14:       score += Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score if comment.user&.base_subscriber?
15:       previous_score = comment.score.to_i
16:       comment.update_columns(score: score, updated_at: Time.current)
17: 
18:       if score >= 3 && comment.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding) && comment.semantic_embedding.nil?
19:         Comments::GenerateEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(comment.id)
20:       end
21: 
22:       enqueue_article_activity_update(previous_score, score) if comment.commentable_type == "Article"
23: 
24:       comment.user&.touch(:last_comment_at)
25: 
26:       # update commentable
27:       commentable = comment.commentable
28: 
29:       commentable.touch(:last_comment_at) if commentable.respond_to?(:last_comment_at)
30:       Comments::Count.call(commentable, recalculate: true) if commentable.is_a?(Article)
31: 
32:       # busting comment cache includes busting commentable cache
33:       Comments::BustCacheWorker.new.perform(comment.id)
34:     end
35: 
36:     private
37: 
38:     attr_reader :comment
39: 
40:     # Emit an article-activity enqueue when the comment crosses the
41:     # "counted" threshold (score > 0). update_columns above bypasses
42:     # callbacks, so the Comment after_commit can't observe this transition.
43:     def enqueue_article_activity_update(previous_score, current_score)
44:       was_counted = previous_score.positive?
45:       is_counted = current_score.to_i.positive?
46:       return if was_counted == is_counted
47: 
48:       action = is_counted ? "create" : "destroy"
49:       payload = { "iso" => comment.created_at.to_date.iso8601 }
50:       Articles::UpdateArticleActivityWorker.perform_async(
51:         comment.commentable_id, "comment", action, payload
52:       )
53:     end
54:   end
55: end

1: # Unpublish posts and delete comments w/ boolean attr (setting deleted: true) to allow revert
2: # Create a corresponding audit_log record
3: module Moderator
4:   class UnpublishAllArticles
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     # @param target_user_id [Integer] the id of the user whose posts are being unpublished
10:     # @param action_user_id [Integer] the id of the user who unpublishes
11:     # @param listener [String] listener for the audit logger
12:     def initialize(target_user_id:, action_user_id:, listener: :admin_api)
13:       @target_user_id = target_user_id
14:       @action_user_id = action_user_id
15:       @listener = listener
16:     end
17: 
18:     delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
19: 
20:     def call
21:       user = User.find_by(id: target_user_id)
22:       return unless user
23: 
24:       target_comments = user.comments.where(deleted: false)
25:       target_articles = user.articles.published
26: 
27:       # cache for logging
28:       target_comments_ids = target_comments.ids
29:       target_articles_ids = target_articles.ids
30: 
31:       target_comments.update(deleted: true)
32: 
33:       target_articles.find_each do |article|
34:         if article.has_frontmatter?
35:           article.body_markdown.sub!(/^published:\s*true\s*$/, "published: false")
36:         end
37:         article.published = false
38:         article.save(validate: false)
39:         clean_up_notifications(article)
40:       end
41: 
42:       payload = {
43:         target_user_id: target_user_id,
44:         target_article_ids: target_articles_ids,
45:         target_comment_ids: target_comments_ids
46:       }
47: 
48:       audit_log(payload)
49:     end
50: 
51:     private
52: 
53:     attr_reader :target_user_id, :action_user_id, :listener
54: 
55:     def clean_up_notifications(article)
56:       Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id,
57:                                                       notifiable_type: "Article",
58:                                                       action: "Published")
59: 
60:       ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article", action: "Published")
61: 
62:       return unless article.comments.exists?
63: 
64:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids, notifiable_type: "Comment")
65:     end
66: 
67:     def audit_log(payload = {})
68:       payload["action"] = listener == :moderator ? "unpublish_all_articles" : "api_user_unpublish"
69:       action_user = User.find_by(id: action_user_id)
70:       Audit::Logger.log(listener, action_user, payload)
71:     end
72:   end
73: end

4:       # @param stories [ActiveRecord::Relation, #detect] pick a

7:       #        story must have a main image
8:       # @return [Article]
9:       #

12:       # @note One might assume that this would query on the
13:       #       `Articles.where(featured: true)` but in my sleuthing,
14:       #       the origin of this method's inner logic never included a
15:       #       consideration for `featured == true`.  Perhaps that

26:             end

1: module Articles
2:   class Suggest
3:     MAX_DEFAULT = 4
4: 
5:     def self.call(article, max: MAX_DEFAULT)
6:       new(article, max: max).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(article, max: MAX_DEFAULT)
10:       @article = article
11:       @max = max
12:       @total_articles_count = Article.published.from_subforem.estimated_count
13:     end
14: 
15:     def call
16:       if cached_tag_list_array.any?
17:         # avoid loading more data if we don't need to
18:         tagged_suggestions = suggestions_by_tag(max: max)
19:         return tagged_suggestions if tagged_suggestions.size == max
20: 
21:         # if there are not enough tagged articles, load other suggestions
22:         # ignoring tagged articles that might be relevant twice, hence avoiding duplicates
23:         num_remaining_needed = max - tagged_suggestions.size
24:         other_articles = other_suggestions(
25:           max: num_remaining_needed,
26:           ids_to_ignore: tagged_suggestions.map(&:id),
27:         )
28:         tagged_suggestions.union(other_articles)
29:       else
30:         other_suggestions
31:       end
32:     end
33: 
34:     private
35: 
36:     attr_reader :article, :max, :total_articles_count
37: 
38:     def other_suggestions(max: MAX_DEFAULT, ids_to_ignore: [])
39:       ids_to_ignore << article.id
40:       Article.published.where(subforem_id: article.subforem_id)
41:         .where(archived: false)
42:         .where("published_at > ?", 3.months.ago)
43:         .where.not(id: ids_to_ignore)
44:         .not_authored_by(article.user_id)
45:         .order(hotness_score: :desc)
46:         .offset(rand(0..offset))
47:         .where("score > ?", Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score)
48:         .select(:id, :title, :path, :cached_tag_list, :cached_user, :main_image, :published, :published_at, :crossposted_at)
49:         .first(max)
50:     end
51: 
52:     def suggestions_by_tag(max: MAX_DEFAULT)
53:       Article
54:         .published.where(subforem_id: article.subforem_id)
55:         .where(archived: false)
56:         .where("published_at > ?", 3.months.ago)
57:         .cached_tagged_with_any(cached_tag_list_array)
58:         .not_authored_by(article.user_id)
59:         .where(tag_suggestion_query)
60:         .order(hotness_score: :desc)
61:         .offset(rand(0..offset))
62:         .where("score > ?", Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score)
63:         .select(:id, :title, :path, :cached_tag_list, :cached_user, :main_image, :published, :published_at, :crossposted_at)
64:         .first(max)
65:     end
66: 
67:     def offset
68:       total_articles_count > 1000 ? 200 : (total_articles_count / 10)
69:     end
70: 
71:     def tag_suggestion_query
72:       # Fore big communities like DEV we can look at organic page views for indicator.
73:       # For smaller communities, we'll a basic score check.
74:       total_articles_count > 1000 ? "organic_page_views_past_month_count > 5" : "score > 1"
75:     end
76: 
77:     def cached_tag_list_array
78:       (article.cached_tag_list || "").split(", ")
79:     end
80:   end
81: end

1: module EdgeCache
2:   class BustComment
3:     def self.call(comment_or_commentable)
4:       comment = comment_or_commentable.is_a?(Comment) ? comment_or_commentable : nil
5:       commentable = comment&.commentable || comment_or_commentable
6:       return unless commentable
7: 
8:       keys = [commentable.record_key, comment&.record_key].compact
9:       fallback_paths = [commentable.path, comment&.path].compact
10:       EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(keys, fallback_paths: fallback_paths)
11:       bust_article_comment(commentable) if commentable.is_a?(Article)
12:       commentable.touch(:last_comment_at) if commentable.respond_to?(:last_comment_at)
13:     end
14: 
15:     # bust commentable if it's an article
16:     def self.bust_article_comment(article)
17:       if Article.published.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
18:         EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call("main_app_home_page", fallback_paths: "/")
19:       end
20: 
21:       return unless article.decorate.discussion?
22: 
23:       EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call("main_app_home_page", fallback_paths: "/")
24:     end
25: 
26:     private_class_method :bust_article_comment
27:   end
28: end

1: module Notifications
2:   class Update
3:     delegate :article_data, :comment_data, :user_data, :organization_data, to: Notifications
4: 
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(notifiable, action = nil)
10:       @notifiable = notifiable
11:       @action = action
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call
15:       return unless [Article, Comment].include?(notifiable.class)
16: 
17:       notifications = Notification.where(
18:         notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
19:         notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
20:         action: action,
21:       )
22:       # as we only select the first notification right after, there is no need
23:       # to load all of them in memory with `.blank?`, thus we choose `.none?`
24:       return if notifications.none?

1: # send notifications about the new reaction
2: module Notifications
3:   module Reactions
4:     class Send
5:       Response = Struct.new(:action, :notification_id)
6: 
7:       def self.call(...)
8:         new(...).call
9:       end
10: 
11:       # @param reaction_data [Hash]
12:       #   * :reactable_id [Integer] - article or comment id
13:       #   * :reactable_type [String] - "Article" or "Comment"
14:       #   * :reactable_user_id [Integer] - user id
15:       #   * :reactable_subforem_id [Integer] - subforem id
16:       # @param receiver [User] or [Organization]
17:       def initialize(reaction_data, receiver)
18:         @reaction = ReactionData.coerce(reaction_data)
19:         @receiver = receiver
20:       end
21: 
22:       delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
23: 
24:       # @return [OpenStruct, #action, #notification_id]
25:       def call
26:         return unless receiver.is_a?(User) || receiver.is_a?(Organization)
27: 
28:         reaction_siblings = Reaction.public_category.where(reactable_id: reaction.reactable_id,
29:                                                            reactable_type: reaction.reactable_type)
30:           .where.not(reactions: { user_id: reaction.reactable_user_id })
31:           .preload(:reactable).includes(:user).where.not(users: { id: nil })
32:           .order("reactions.created_at" => :desc)
33: 
34:         aggregated_reaction_siblings = reaction_siblings.map do |reaction|
35:           { category: reaction.category, created_at: reaction.created_at, user: user_data(reaction.user) }
36:         end
37: 
38:         notification_params = {
39:           notifiable_type: reaction.reactable_type,
40:           notifiable_id: reaction.reactable_id,
41:           subforem_id: reaction.reactable_subforem_id,
42:           action: "Reaction"
43:         }
44:         case receiver
45:         when User
46:           notification_params[:user_id] = receiver.id
47:         when Organization
48:           notification_params[:organization_id] = receiver.id
49:         end
50: 
51:         if aggregated_reaction_siblings.empty?
52:           Notification.where(notification_params).delete_all
53:           Response.new(:deleted)
54:         else
55:           recent_reaction = reaction_siblings.first
56: 
57:           json_data = reaction_json_data(recent_reaction, aggregated_reaction_siblings)
58: 
59:           notification = Notification.find_or_initialize_by(notification_params)
60: 
61:           previous_siblings_size = notification.json_data&.dig("reaction", "aggregated_siblings")&.size || 0
62: 
63:           notification.json_data = json_data
64:           notification.notified_at = Time.current
65:           notification.read = false if json_data[:reaction][:aggregated_siblings].size > previous_siblings_size
66: 
67:           notification_id = save_notification(notification_params, notification)
68: 
69:           Response.new(:saved, notification_id)
70:         end
71:       end
72: 
73:       private
74: 
75:       attr_reader :reaction, :receiver
76: 
77:       # when a notification exists in the DB already it's safe to just save it,
78:       # when it doesn't, there could be a race condition when 2 jobs try to create
79:       # duplicate notifications concurrently, in this case upsert is used to
80:       # rely on PostgreSQL constraints, thus we use `.upsert`
81:       def save_notification(params, notification)
82:         if notification.persisted?
83:           notification.save!
84:           notification.id
85:         else
86:           # in the upsert, we are only interested in updating the following columns:
87:           # json_data, notified_at, read and the notification params used by `.find_or_initialize_by`
88:           upsert_columns = %w[json_data notified_at read]
89:           upsert_attributes = notification.attributes.select { |k| upsert_columns.include?(k) }
90:           upsert_attributes.merge!(params)
91: 
92:           # unfortunately Rails requires the timestamps to be present even if unused in case of upsert
93:           # see <https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/35493>
94:           now = Time.current
95:           upsert_attributes["created_at"] = upsert_attributes["updated_at"] = now
96: 
97:           # we also need to select the correct index to let PostgreSQL know
98:           # how to determine conflict on rows
99:           upsert_index = choose_upsert_index(notification)
100: 
101:           upsert_result = Notification.upsert(
102:             upsert_attributes,
103:             unique_by: upsert_index,
104:             returning: %i[id],
105:           )
106: 
107:           upsert_result.to_a.first["id"]
108:         end
109:       end
110: 
111:       def choose_upsert_index(notification)
112:         # if none of these is present, we use the two columns for the upsert
113:         return %i[notifiable_id notifiable_type] unless
114:           notification.action? || notification.user_id? || notification.organization_id?
115: 
116:         if notification.action?
117:           return :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_and_action_not_null if notification.user_id?
118: 
119:           :index_notifications_on_org_notifiable_and_action_not_null
120:         else

1: class UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker
2:   include Sidekiq::Job
3:   sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
4: 
5:   def perform(user_id, article_id, blend_factor = 0.2)
6:     blend_factor = blend_factor.to_f.clamp(0.0, 1.0)
7:     article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
8:     return unless article && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding) && article.semantic_embedding.present?
9: 
10:     article_vector = article.semantic_embedding.to_a
11:     return unless article_vector.length == 768
12: 
13:     UserActivity.transaction do
14:       user_activity = UserActivity.lock.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id)
15: 
16:       if user_activity.respond_to?(:interest_embedding)
17:         current_vector = user_activity.interest_embedding&.to_a
18: 
19:         new_vector = if current_vector.blank? || current_vector.length != 768
20:                        article_vector
21:                      else
22:                        # Blend vectors using Exponential Moving Average
23:                        current_vector.zip(article_vector).map do |c, a|
24:                          (c * (1 - blend_factor)) + (a * blend_factor)
25:                        end
26:                      end
27: 
28:         user_activity.update_column(:interest_embedding, new_vector)
29:       end
30:     end
31:   end
32: end

<content>
1: class LiquidEmbedExtractor
2:   # We implemented a non-rendering parser scanning body_markdown directly to avoid instantiating Liquid tags.
3:   # This prevents background sync jobs from invoking Pundit auth, HTTP network requests (like UnifiedEmbed fetching OpenGraph data),
4:   # or halting the extraction entirely if a single tag fails Liquid::Template context validations.
5:   LIQUID_TAG_REGEX = /\{%\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s+(.*?)\s*%\}/
6: 
7:   def self.extract(record)
8:     content = record.respond_to?(:body_markdown) ? record.body_markdown : nil
9:     return [] if content.blank?
10: 
11:     # Strip codeblocks to reliably extract active tags only natively
12:     stripped_content = content.to_s.gsub(/```.*?```/m, "").gsub(/`.*?`/, "")
13: 
14:     tags = []
15:     stripped_content.scan(LIQUID_TAG_REGEX).each do |match|
16:       tag_name = match[0].downcase
17:       options = match[1].strip
18:       url = options.split(" ").first || options
19: 
20:       ref_type, ref_id = derive_reference(tag_name, url)
21: 
22:       tags << {
23:         tag_name: tag_name,
24:         url: url,
25:         options: options,
26:         referenced_type: ref_type,
27:         referenced_id: ref_id
28:       }
29:     end
30: 
31:     tags.uniq { |t| [t[:tag_name], t[:url], t[:options]] }
32:   end
33: 
34:   def self.derive_reference(tag_name, identifier)
35:     case tag_name
36:     when "user"
37:       user = User.find_by(username: identifier)
38:       [user&.class&.name, user&.id]
39:     when "comment"
40:       comment = Comment.find_by(id_code: identifier)
41:       [comment&.class&.name, comment&.id]
42:     when "tag"
43:       tag = Tag.find_by(name: identifier)
44:       [tag&.class&.name, tag&.id]
45:     when "podcast"
46:       podcast = Podcast.find_by(slug: identifier)
47:       [podcast&.class&.name, podcast&.id]
48:     when "organization", "org_posts"
49:       org = Organization.find_by(slug: identifier)
50:       [org&.class&.name, org&.id]
51:     when "embed"
52:       # If tag is explicitly embed, map internal Article urls without running ActionDispatch logic natively.
53:       app_domain = Settings::General.app_domain || "localhost:#{URL.dev_port}"
54:       if identifier.include?(app_domain)
55:         begin
56:           path = URI.parse(identifier).path
57:           if path.present?
58:             segments = path.split("/")
59:             if segments.size >= 3
60:               article = Article.joins(:user).find_by(users: { username: segments[1] }, slug: segments[2])
61:               return [article.class.name, article.id] if article
62:             end
63:           end
64:         rescue URI::InvalidURIError
65:           # safely ignore invalid payload URLs dynamically
66:         end
67:       end
68:       [nil, nil]
69:     else
70:       [nil, nil]
71:     end
72:   end
73: end

1: module Articles
2:   module Feeds
3:     TIME_AGO_MAX = Rails.env.production? ? 10.days.ago : 90.days.ago
4: 
5:     class Custom
6:       def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil, feed_config: nil)
7:         @user = user
8:         @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
9:         @page = [page, 1].max
10:         @tag = tag
11:         @feed_config = feed_config
12:       end
13: 
14:       def default_home_feed(**_kwargs)
15:         return [] if @feed_config.nil? || @user.nil?
16:         
17:         execute_feed_query
18:       end
19: 
20:       private
21: 
22:       def execute_feed_query
23:         # Optimize lookback calculation - cache the result
24:         # Note: Partial indexes are optimized for 7-day lookback (covers 95%+ of queries)
25:         # If you change this, consider updating the partial indexes in the migration
26:         lookback_setting = Settings::UserExperience.feed_lookback_days.to_i
27:         lookback = lookback_setting.positive? ? lookback_setting.days.ago : TIME_AGO_MAX
28:         
29:         # Pre-calculate user-specific data to avoid repeated database calls
30:         user_data = preload_user_data
31:         
32:         # Build optimized base query with better index usage
33:         articles = build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
34:         
35:         # Apply user-specific filters early in the query
36:         articles = apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
37:         
38:         # Apply subforem-specific filters
39:         articles = apply_subforem_filters(articles)
40:         
41:         # Apply weighted shuffle if needed
42:         articles = weighted_shuffle(articles, @feed_config.shuffle_weight) if @feed_config.shuffle_weight.positive?
43:         
44:         # Randomly shuffle top 5 articles if all articles are recent (within a week)
45:         articles = shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
46:         
47:         articles
48:       end
49: 
50: 
51: 
52:       def preload_user_data
53:         {
54:           blocked_user_ids: UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id),
55:           hidden_tags: @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names,
56:           user_activity: @user.user_activity
57:         }
58:       end
59: 
60:       def build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
61:         # Use a more efficient query structure with better index hints
62:         base_query = Article.published
63:           .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
64:           .where("articles.published_at > ?", lookback)
65:           .select("articles.*, (#{score_sql_method}) as computed_score")
66:           .order(Arel.sql("computed_score DESC"))
67:           .limit(@number_of_articles)
68:           .offset((@page - 1) * @number_of_articles)
69:           .limited_column_select
70:           .includes(:subforem) # Only include essential associations
71:           .from_subforem
72: 
73:         # Include necessary associations for performance
74:         base_query = base_query.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes, top_comments: :user)
75:         
76:         base_query
77:       end
78: 
79:       def score_sql_method
80:         @feed_config.score_sql(@user)
81:       end
82: 
83: 
84:       def apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
85:         # Apply user-specific filters early to reduce dataset size
86:         if user_data[:blocked_user_ids].any?
87:           articles = articles.where.not(user_id: user_data[:blocked_user_ids])
88:         end
89:         
90:         if user_data[:hidden_tags].any?
91:           articles = if ENV["OPTIMIZED_FEED_TAGS_QUERY"] == "true"
92:                        articles.where.not("articles.tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", user_data[:hidden_tags].map(&:to_s))
93:                      else
94:                        articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(user_data[:hidden_tags])
95:                      end
96:         end
97:         
98:         articles
99:       end
100: 
101:       def apply_subforem_filters(articles)
102:         # Apply subforem-specific filters
103:         if RequestStore.store[:subforem_id] == RequestStore.store[:root_subforem_id]
104:           articles = articles.where(type_of: :full_post)
105:         end
106:         
107:         articles
108:       end
109: 
110:       def weighted_shuffle(arr, shuffle_weight)
111:         # Each element gets a new sort key: its original index plus a random offset.
112:         # We choose the random offset uniformly from -2*shuffle_weight to 2*shuffle_weight.
113:         # The average absolute offset then is (2*shuffle_weight)/2 = shuffle_weight.
114:         arr.each_with_index.sort_by do |item, index|
115:           index + (rand * (4 * shuffle_weight) - 2 * shuffle_weight)
116:         end.map(&:first)
117:       end
118: 
119:       def shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
120:         return articles if articles.empty?
